revision.text:
[[ja:阿刀神社]] {{Expand Japanese|阿刀神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ato Shrine | native_name = 阿刀神社 | image = Ato-jinja shaden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ato Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.020589|135.691996}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu|ar|أماتيراسو|ary|أماطيراسو|az|Amaterasu|bcl|Amaterasu|be_x_old|Аматэрасу|be|Аматэрасу|bew|Amatérasu|bg|Аматерасу|ca|Amaterasu|cs|Amaterasu|da|Amaterasu|de|Amaterasu|el|Αματεράσου|en|Amaterasu|eo|Amaterasu|es|Amaterasu|et|Amaterasu|eu|Amaterasu|fa|آماتراسو|fi|Amaterasu|fr|Amaterasu|gl|Amaterasu|he|אמטראסו|hi|अमातेरसु|hr|Amaterasu|hu|Amateraszu Ómikami|hy|Ամատերասու Օմիկամի|id|Amaterasu|it|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|jaquote|アマテラス|jv|Amaterasu|ka|ამატერასუ|ko|아마테라스 오미카미|la|Amaterasu|lb|Amaterasu|lt|Amaterasu|lv|Amaterasu|min|Amaterasu|ml|അമതേരസു|mni|ꯑꯃꯇꯦꯔꯁꯨ ꯑꯣꯃꯤꯀꯃꯤ|ms|Amaterasu|nl|Amaterasu|nn|Amaterasu|no|Amaterasu|or|ଆମାତେରାଶୁ|pa|ਆਮਾਤੇਰਸ|pl|Amaterasu|pt|Amaterasu|ro|Amaterasu|ru|Аматэрасу|sco|Amaterasu|sh|Amaterasu|simple|Amaterasu Ōmikami|sk|Amaterasu Ómikami|sl|Amaterasu|sr|Аматерасу|su|Amaterasu|sv|Amaterasu|ta|அமதெரசு|th|อามาเตราซุ|tl|Amaterasu|tr|Amaterasu|uk|Аматерасу|uz|Amaterasu|vi|Amaterasu|wuu|天照大神|zh_min_nan|Amaterasu|zh_yue|天照大神|zh|天照大神|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ato Shrine'''|阿刀神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamashiro Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11467693|ltq=Q11467693|qid=Q11467693|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province}} ** Series ordinal: 23 ** P155: {{ill|Wochikahano Ochikaha Shrine|lt=Wochikahano Ochikaha Shrine|qid=Q135038757|1=Wochikahano Ochikaha Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q710302|ltq=Q710302|qid=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q580186|ltq=Q580186|qid=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|lt=Yamashiro Province|cs|Provincie Jamaširo|de|Provinz Yamashiro|en|Yamashiro Province|es|Provincia de Yamashiro|fa|ولایت یاماشیرو|fr|Province de Yamashiro|gl|Provincia de Yamashiro|hak|Yamashiro-koet|id|Provinsi Yamashiro|it|Yamashiro (provincia)|ja|山城国|ko|야마시로국|lt|Jamaširo žemė|ms|Yamashiro Province|nl|Yamashiro (provincie)|pt|Província de Yamashiro|ru|Ямасиро (провинция)|simple|Yamashiro Province|sk|Jamaširo (provincia)|sr|Провинција Јамаширо|tr|Yamashiro (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ямашіро|wuu|山城国|zh_min_nan|Yamasiro-kok|zh_yue|山城國|zh|山城國|qid=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} * {{ill|Q13579879|ltq=Q13579879|qid=Q13579879|1=Kadono district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.020589|135.691996}} == image (P18) == * Ato-jinja shaden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q455602|ltq=Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180592 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11545372|ltq=Q11545372|qid=Q11545372|1=Senior Sixth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8130005001477 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市右京区嵯峨広沢南野町80', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '京都府京都市右京区嵯峨南野町', 'language': 'ja'} == located on street (P669) == * {{ill|Marutamachi Street|lt=Marutamachi Street|fr|Marutamachi-dōri|ja|丸太町通|zh|丸太町通|qid=Q11368086|1=Marutamachi Street}} [[JA:阿刀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17191533}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ato Shrine |Image = [[File:Ato-jinja shaden.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and shrine building |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Ukyo Ward|Q580186|qid=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} 80 Saga Hirosawa Minaminocho |Coordinates = {{coord|35|1|14.10|N|135|41|31.16|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ato Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Estimated) early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}}{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Ato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{List of coordinates}} '''Ato Shrine''' (あとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Saga Hirosawa Minaminocho, {{ill|lt=Ukyo Ward|Q580186|qid=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === According to the “Shrine Register” of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|qid=Q7816|1=1883}}), the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. On the other hand, the “Jingishi Shiryō” states that the enshrined deity is Ato no Sukune’s ancestral deity, Aji Nuta no Mikoto ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ajinuta no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}){{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. There is also a theory that the shrine name “Ato” derives from “Atsu,” that is, “Yatsu (valley),” through phonetic change, and that the enshrined deity is a water deity (= an agricultural deity){{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. === History === === Overview === [[File:Atobe-jinja (Yao), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Atobe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}})}}]] As suggested by the shrine name “Ato,” this shrine is regarded as the {{ill|lt=clan tutelary shrine|Q767655|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami shrine|1=Ujigami}} of the ancient clan {{ill|lt=Ato clan|Q17191532|qid=Q17191532|1=Ato clan}}{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Regarding the Ato clan, the “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” ({{ill|lt=815|Q29917|qid=Q29917|1=815}}) records “Ato no Sukune” and “Ato no Muraji” in Yamashiro Province, stating that they were branch families of the {{ill|lt=Isonokami clan|UNKNOWN|lt=Isonokami no Ason}} ({{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}) and descendants of Aji Nuta no Mikoto, a grandson of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The original base of the Ato clan was Atobe in {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|qid=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|qid=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} (around present-day {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atobe Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}), but it is said that with the relocation of the capital to {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō|Q751907|qid=Q751907|1=Heian-kyō}}, they migrated and transferred their ancestral deity to this area{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In the {{ill|lt=Six National Histories|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|qid=Q23872|1=866}})“Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,” Jōgan 8 (866), intercalary 3rd month, 7th day., a divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower was bestowed upon what appears to be the Ato deity, recorded as “Furui-gami”{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In addition, the “{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|qid=Q1077029|1=Nihon Kōki}}”“Nihon Kōki,” Enryaku 23 (804), 2nd month, 5th day. states that “the ritual implements of {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|qid=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} in Yamato Province were transferred to Katsuno District, Yamashiro Province,” and it is said that this place was a small shrine called “Shingū no Yashiro” that existed northwest of Ato Shrine (Saga Shingūcho) until the Meiji period{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}} (no longer extant{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}}). In the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}” {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi era|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), “Ato Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Katsuno District|Q13579879|qid=Q13579879|1=Kadono district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, and it is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|qid=Q7773|1=1877}}), March, under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. At present, only a small shrine remains. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 8 ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|qid=Q23872|1=866}}), intercalary 3rd month, 7th day: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”). The notation is “Furui-gami,” and manuscripts contain a marginal note “Ato,” which is taken to indicate this shrine{{Sfn|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="Original"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.)|author=Kasai Yamato|year=1979|chapter=Ato Shrine|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 1|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ato Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Ato Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 27: Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490271|ref={{Harvid|Ato Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ato clan|title=Dictionary of Names of Ancient Japanese Clans, Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|qid=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|isbn=9784642014588|ref=}} === Related items === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atobe Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Yao City, Osaka Prefecture, {{coord|34|37|4.88|N|135|34|43.40|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|name=Birthplace of the Ato clan / Original shrine: Atobe Shrine}}) – The birthplace of the Ato clan. A Shikinaisha in Shibukawa District, Kawachi Province, and regarded as the original shrine of Ato Shrine. === External links === * [http://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=10&ManageCode=342 Shikinaisha Ato Shrine] – Kyoto City Industry and Tourism Bureau “Kyoto Tourism Navi” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ato clan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ato clan]]
